name: refactor-complete-messaging-dashboard-alignment
title: Complete Messaging Dashboard alignment with Jobs Dashboard
description: Complete the Messaging Dashboard alignment: remove dead code, add ECharts charts, port exception rendering, enhance message detail dialog, redesign footer, add nav badges, K8s namespace support, subscriber syntax highlighting, and UX polish

userStories.title: Remove dead code: i18n, Tailwind, unused types/configs [S]
userStories.description: Sequential: (1) Remove vue-i18n — uninstall from package.json, delete src/i18n/ directory, remove i18n plugin from main.ts, remove any $t() or t() calls if any exist. (2) Remove Tailwind — uninstall tailwindcss from package.json, delete tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js, remove @tailwind directives from src/assets/main.css. (3) Delete src/config/auth.config.ts (exported but never imported). (4) Remove unused type exports from src/services/http.ts: ApiResponse, PaginatedResponse, ErrorResponse (declared but never imported). (5) Remove hideForAuthenticated meta from router (declared but guard never checks it). (6) Verify build.
acceptanceCriteria: No vue-i18n dependency in package.json, no i18n/ directory, no $t() calls
No tailwindcss dependency, no tailwind.config.js, no postcss.config.js, no @tailwind directives in main.css
auth.config.ts deleted, unused type exports removed from http.ts
Frontend builds with zero errors
notes: vue-i18n translations are registered in main.ts but zero $t() calls exist in any template — completely unused
Tailwind imported via 3 @tailwind directives but zero Tailwind classes used — only Vuetify utility classes throughout

userStories.title: Redesign footer — system info + switched node + fixed height [S]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Redesign DashboardLayout.vue footer to: (1) Show system info badges: messaging type+version, broker name, storage name (from /api/meta). Match old dashboard pattern: secondary badges inline. (2) Show 'Switched Node' warning badge when messaging.node cookie is set (read cookie, show node name). (3) Make footer fixed height (~40px) and always visible at bottom. (4) Remove system info section from Dashboard.vue main content area. (5) Fetch meta once on app init and cache (via messagingStore or a dedicated ref in layout).
acceptanceCriteria: Footer displays messaging type+version, broker name, storage name as inline badges
Footer shows 'Switched Node: <name>' warning badge when messaging.node cookie is set
Footer has fixed height (~40px) and is always visible at bottom of layout
System info section removed from Dashboard.vue main content area
Meta data fetched once and cached (not re-fetched on every page navigation)
notes: Old dashboard Footer.vue read messaging.node cookie via manual cookie parsing and showed warning badge with node name

userStories.title: Add 404 catch-all route [XS]
userStories.description: Sequential: add /:pathMatch(.*)*  catch-all route to router -> redirect to Dashboard or show minimal 'Page not found' message
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to an unknown path (e.g. /nonexistent) does not render a blank page
User is redirected to Dashboard or shown a 'Page not found' message

userStories.title: Add badge counts to navigation links [S]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Add badge counts to DashboardLayout.vue nav links matching old dashboard pattern: (1) Published link: red badge with publishedFailed count. (2) Received link: red badge with receivedFailed count. (3) Subscribers link: info badge with subscribers count. (4) Nodes link: badge with servers count. Counts come from /api/stats which is already polled. Wire via messagingStore (US-005) or a shared reactive ref. Badges should use v-badge or append v-chip to nav buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Published nav link shows red badge with publishedFailed count (hidden when 0)
Received nav link shows red badge with receivedFailed count (hidden when 0)
Subscribers nav link shows info badge with subscriber count
Nodes nav link shows badge with server count
Badge counts update automatically when polling refreshes stats
notes: Old dashboard Navigation.vue used b-badge on each nav item with counts from Vuex metrics store
Depends on US-005 (messagingStore) for shared stats state — or can use a simpler shared reactive ref

userStories.title: Create messagingStore (Pinia) for dashboard state [S]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Create a Pinia store to manage dashboard state shared across components: stats (publishedSucceeded, publishedFailed, publishedDelayed, receivedSucceeded, receivedFailed, subscribers, servers), meta (messaging/broker/storage info), realtimeMetrics, and metricsHistory. Move polling from Dashboard.vue into the store — start on first subscriber, stop when no subscribers remain. Cache data across navigations. Expose computed getters for chart-ready data and nav badge counts.
acceptanceCriteria: messagingStore.ts created with stats, meta, realtimeMetrics, metricsHistory state
Polling lifecycle managed by the store (startPolling/stopPolling actions)
Dashboard.vue uses the store instead of local reactive state
Nav badge counts (US-004) and footer meta (US-002) can read from this store
Navigating away and back shows cached data immediately, polling resumes
notes: Old dashboard used Vuex store with metric state polled every 2s from App.vue
Stats are polled from /api/stats; meta from /api/meta (once); realtime from /api/metrics-realtime; history from /api/metrics-history (once)

userStories.title: Add ECharts dashboard charts (line + pie) [L]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. (1) Add echarts + vue-echarts to package.json. (2) Create real-time metrics line chart — dual Y-axis matching old dashboard: left axis 'Rate (TPS)' for published/sec (green) and subscribed/sec (red); right axis 'Elapsed Time (ms)' for subscriber latency (blue scatter). 300 data points, auto-updating via polling. (3) Create hourly history chart — 4 series as filled area: Publish Succeeded (green), Received Succeeded (blue), Publish Failed (red), Received Failed (yellow/orange). Matches old uPlot chart style. (4) Create success/failure pie chart from /api/stats. (5) Match Jobs Dashboard dark theme: transparent bg, #e0e0e0 text, dashed grid lines rgba(255,255,255,0.05), dark tooltips rgba(42,42,42,0.95). Use tree-shaking imports (CanvasRenderer, LineChart, PieChart, ScatterChart + required components). Remove raw JSON dump and flat history table from Dashboard.vue.
acceptanceCriteria: echarts and vue-echarts added to package.json with tree-shaking imports
Real-time metrics chart renders dual Y-axis: TPS rate (left, 2 line series) + elapsed time (right, scatter)
Hourly history chart renders 4 filled area series matching old uPlot chart
Success/failure pie chart renders from /api/stats data
Charts use dark theme matching Jobs Dashboard palette
Raw JSON metrics dump and flat history table removed from Dashboard.vue
Charts auto-update when polling refreshes data in messagingStore
notes: Old dashboard had uPlot with dual Y-axis: TPS rate (left) + elapsed ms (right). Green=publish, red=consume, blue scatter=latency
Old 24h history used filled area charts: green=pub success, blue=recv success, red=pub fail, yellow=recv fail
Jobs Dashboard ECharts setup pattern in TimeJobCharts.vue lines 92-116

userStories.title: Enrich message detail backend endpoints [S]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Modify GET /api/published/message/{id} and GET /api/received/message/{id} to return a DTO with metadata alongside content. Currently returns message.Content (string only). New shape: { id, name, content, added, expiresAt, retries, statusName, version }. For received messages, also include group. This enables the frontend MessageDetailDialog to show metadata and identify failed messages for exception rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/published/message/{id} returns DTO with id, name, content, added, expiresAt, retries, statusName, version
GET /api/received/message/{id} returns same DTO shape plus group field
Existing list endpoint response shape unchanged (non-breaking)

userStories.title: Port ConfirmDialog exception/code rendering from Jobs [M]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. Port the Jobs Dashboard ConfirmDialog exception rendering into the Messaging ConfirmDialog. Add to ConfirmDialogProps: isException (bool), isCode (bool), showWarningAlert (bool), warningAlertMessage (string). Add structured exception parsing: try JSON.parse, detect Message/StackTrace fields (PascalCase or camelCase), build StructuredException/GenericException/PlainException types. Render: (1) Error message box (red bg). (2) Collapsible Stack Trace section (blue left border, expand/collapse with animation). (3) Additional Information section: Source, HelpLink (as clickable anchor), Data (as JSON pre block), InnerException. (4) Collapsible Raw Exception Data section (orange left border). (5) isCode mode for plain code/JSON display. Port all styles from Jobs ConfirmDialog (lines 307-648).
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmDialogProps has isException, isCode, showWarningAlert, warningAlertMessage fields
Structured exception parsing handles both PascalCase (Message, StackTrace) and camelCase (message, stackTrace)
Exception view shows: error message box, collapsible stack trace, additional info (source, help link, data), collapsible raw data
isCode mode renders content in monospace pre block
All sections have expand/collapse with animation matching Jobs pattern
Styles match Jobs dark theme (section headers with colored left borders, gradient backgrounds)
notes: Jobs ConfirmDialog: type defs lines 22-44, parsing logic lines 77-195, template lines 198-305, styles lines 307-648
Exception types: StructuredException (message + stackTrace + source + helpLink + data + innerException), GenericException (any JSON object), PlainException (raw string)

userStories.title: Enhance MessageDetailDialog — metadata, exceptions, json-bigint [M]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. (1) Add json-bigint to package.json — use for parsing message content to prevent precision loss for IDs > 2^53. (2) Expand MessageDetailDialog to accept full message DTO (from US-007). (3) Add metadata header section: name, status chip (color-coded by statusName), added date, expires at, retries count, version. For received messages also show group. (4) For failed messages (statusName === 'Failed'), detect if content contains a serialized exception and render using the exception pattern from US-008 (collapsible stack trace, source, help link, data, inner exception). (5) Add copy-to-clipboard button for message content. (6) Handle non-JSON content gracefully (display as plain text with 'Text content' indicator). (7) Update Published.vue and Received.vue to pass full message DTO to the dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: json-bigint added to package.json and used for message content parsing
Dialog shows metadata header: name, status chip (color-coded), added date, expires at, retries, version
Failed messages show structured exception rendering with collapsible stack trace, source, help link, data, inner exception sections
Copy-to-clipboard button copies message content to clipboard with success feedback
Non-JSON content displays as plain text without parse errors
Published.vue and Received.vue pass full message DTO to dialog (not just content string)
notes: Old dashboard used vue-json-pretty + json-bigint for message display — json-bigint prevents Number precision loss
Exception rendering should reuse the parsing pattern from US-008 ConfirmDialog, or extract a shared composable

userStories.title: Add missing stat cards (Subscribers, PublishedDelayed) [S]
userStories.description: Sequential: map Subscribers and PublishedDelayed from /api/stats response in messagingStore -> add stat cards to Dashboard.vue -> verify rendering. Total stat cards: 7 (was 5).
acceptanceCriteria: Subscribers count stat card displayed on Dashboard page
PublishedDelayed count stat card displayed on Dashboard page
Values sourced from /api/stats response (already returned by backend)

userStories.title: Published/Received UX — tab counts, delayed labels, refresh [M]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. (1) Add status counts as badges on Published/Received tab labels — Succeeded (count), Failed (count, red), Delayed (count, warning, Published only). Counts from /api/stats via messagingStore. (2) In Published.vue: when viewing 'Delayed' tab, change 'Expires At' column header to 'Delayed Publish Time' and 'Requeue' button label to 'Immediately Publish'. Add tooltip on Delayed tab: 'Only shows delay time more than 1 minute messages here...' (3) Add manual refresh button to Published.vue and Received.vue toolbar — consistent with Subscribers/Nodes page pattern (outlined, primary, mdi-refresh icon, loading state). (4) Add page size 500 option to usePagination defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Published tabs show badge counts per status (Succeeded, Failed in red, Delayed in warning)
Received tabs show badge counts per status (Succeeded, Failed in red)
Published 'Delayed' tab: 'Expires At' header -> 'Delayed Publish Time', 'Requeue' -> 'Immediately Publish'
Delayed tab has info tooltip explaining delay threshold
Manual refresh button on both Published.vue and Received.vue with loading state
Page size options include 500
notes: Old dashboard Published.vue had Delayed tooltip: 'Only show delay time more than 1 minute messages here, the status of shorter than 1 minute messages name is Queued, you can check it in the database'
Old dashboard changed Requeue -> 'Immediately Publish' and Expires -> 'Delayed Publish Time' when viewing delayed status

userStories.title: Nodes page — K8s namespace selector, ping all, active highlight [M]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. (1) Add K8s namespace selector dropdown — fetch namespaces from GET /list-ns on mount. When a namespace is selected, fetch services from GET /list-svc/{namespace}. Persist selected namespace to messaging.node.ns cookie. Only show dropdown when namespace list is non-empty. (2) Add 'Ping All' button — sequentially ping all visible nodes, updating latency per-node as results arrive. Show loading spinner on button. (3) Highlight active node — read messaging.node cookie, apply distinct row styling (e.g. dark variant or left border) to the matching node row. (4) On node switch: set both messaging.node and messaging.node.ns cookies (the latter for K8s namespace). Show success alert.
acceptanceCriteria: Namespace dropdown shown when /list-ns returns namespaces, hidden otherwise
Selecting a namespace fetches services via /list-svc/{ns} and updates node list
Selected namespace persisted to messaging.node.ns cookie
Ping All button sequentially pings all visible nodes with per-node latency update
Active node (from messaging.node cookie) has distinct row styling
Node switch sets both messaging.node and messaging.node.ns cookies
notes: Old Nodes.vue: b-form-select for namespaces, speedometer icon for Ping All, dark row variant for active node
Backend endpoints /list-ns and /list-svc/{namespace} already exist — no backend changes needed
Old dashboard used messaging.node and messaging.node.ns cookies

userStories.title: Subscriber method syntax highlighting [S]
userStories.description: TDD order: write failing test -> implement minimal code -> verify PASS. The old Subscriber page rendered C# method signatures with color-coded syntax highlighting: types in teal (rgb(43,145,175)), keywords in blue (rgb(0,0,255)), strings in dark red (rgb(163,21,21)), comments in green (rgb(0,128,0)). The backend returns methodEscaped which contains pre-rendered HTML from HtmlHelper.MethodEscaped() with <span class='type'>, <span class='keyword'>, etc. Currently the new Subscribers.vue renders methodEscaped as plain text in a <code> tag. Fix: render methodEscaped as HTML (v-html) with the corresponding CSS classes for syntax colors. Ensure XSS safety — the HTML comes from the backend (trusted), but sanitize if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Method signatures render with syntax highlighting: types in teal, keywords in blue, strings in dark red, comments in green
methodEscaped HTML rendered via v-html with corresponding CSS classes
Method display matches old dashboard pattern (multi-line: ImplTypeName on first line, method signature on second)
notes: Backend HtmlHelper.MethodEscaped() returns pre-rendered HTML with <span class='type'>, <span class='keyword'>, <span class='string'>, <span class='comment'>
Old CSS: .type{color:rgb(43,145,175)} .keyword{color:rgb(0,0,255)} .string{color:rgb(163,21,21)} .comment{color:rgb(0,128,0)}

userStories.title: Final build verification and dist cleanup [XS]
userStories.description: Sequential: run npm run build -> verify zero errors -> verify no stale dist artifacts from old build -> run npm run type-check if available -> smoke test index.html loads
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build completes with zero errors and zero warnings
Built dist/ contains only current build artifacts (no stale files from old builds)

findings: Jobs Dashboard uses echarts + vue-echarts with tree-shaking imports (CanvasRenderer, LineChart, PieChart + components) — see TimeJobCharts.vue:92-116
Jobs Dashboard dark theme chart palette: transparent bg, #e0e0e0 text, rgba(42,42,42,0.95) tooltip bg, rgba(255,255,255,0.05) grid lines, DataZoom slider

Jobs ConfirmDialog: StructuredException/GenericException/PlainException types (lines 22-44), parsing logic handles both PascalCase and camelCase (lines 77-128), collapsible sections with animation (lines 198-305), full dark theme styles (lines 307-648)
Messaging /api/metrics-realtime returns CircularBuffer<int?>[4]: timestamps, published/s, subscribed/s, latency — 300 data points at 1s granularity
Messaging /api/metrics-history returns {DayHour[], PublishSuccessed[], PublishFailed[], SubscribeSuccessed[], SubscribeFailed[]} — hourly aggregates, 10min cached
Old dashboard had uPlot dual Y-axis chart: TPS rate (left, green=publish, red=consume) + elapsed ms (right, blue scatter=latency). History used filled area charts
Old dashboard Navigation.vue had b-badge on each nav item: publishedFailed (danger), receivedFailed (danger), subscribers (info), servers (light)
Old dashboard Footer.vue showed 4 badges: switched node (warning, from cookie), messaging name+version, storage, broker
Old dashboard Published.vue changed 'Expires' -> 'Delayed Publish Time' and 'Requeue' -> 'Immediately Publish' for delayed status, had tooltip on Delayed tab
Old dashboard Nodes.vue had K8s namespace dropdown (GET /list-ns, GET /list-svc/{ns}), Ping All button, active node dark row variant
Old Subscriber.vue rendered methodEscaped as HTML with C# syntax highlighting: .type(teal), .keyword(blue), .string(red), .comment(green)
Old dashboard used json-bigint for message content parsing to prevent precision loss on large message IDs
Messaging has vue-i18n installed with en/ar locales but zero $t() calls — completely unused dead code
Tailwind CSS installed and @tailwind directives in main.css but zero Tailwind classes used in any component
auth.config.ts exported but never imported; ApiResponse/PaginatedResponse/ErrorResponse types declared but never imported
